title: Star Wars: The Last of the Jedi
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}}
{{Otheruses|The Last Jedi}}
'''''Star Wars: The Last of the Jedi''''' is a series of ten young-reader novels written by [[Jude Watson]] following [[Ferus Olin]] in the period of time between [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].

Beginning with [[The Last of the Jedi: Return of the Dark Side|''Return of the Dark Side'']], the series was billed on the covers of the books as simply ''Last of the Jedi''. The [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] release of the series bills it as ''Last of the Jedi'' for all titles.

==Development==
Early in development, the ''Last of the Jedi'' series was envisioned as the continuing adventures of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], but [[Lucasfilm]] ultimately vetoed this concept, reasoning that Kenobi would never venture far from [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] unless exceptional circumstances demanded it.<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref>

==Continuity==
Almost a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] has passed between the events of [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|Episode III]] and the start of this series. The heavily [[Armor/Legends|armored]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[D'harhan]] was first introduced in ''[[The Mandalorian Armor]]'' ([[1998]]), book one of ''[[The Bounty Hunter Wars]]'' trilogy by [[K. W. Jeter]], set after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="TERC" />
